LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.StartWithHonor.Help:
Chosen pawns will start with a specified amount of Honor with the chosen faction.
"Apply Titles" will automatically consume Honor to bestow the proper titles. If disabled, the bestowing ceremonies still have to happen.
Required starting Honor for titles: Freeholder: 1 Novice: 7 Acolyte: 13 Knight/Dame: 21 Praetor: 31 Baron/Baroness: 45 Archon: 65
This will not add Psylinks. For that, use the "Forced Psylink Level" part.
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.StartWithHonor.Summ: {0} have a {1} chance to start with {2} Honor with {3}
{0} have a {1} chance to start with {2} Honor with {3} (with bestowed titles)
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.FactionNaturalGoodw:
Change a faction's natural goodwill to a specified amount.
This affects their starting goodwill, and what value they will trend towards if left alone.
Ally: 75 or higher Neutral: Between -74 and 74 (inclusive) Hostile: -75 or lower
Natural goodwill of faction {0} is set to {1}.
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.FactionForcedGoodwi:
Permanently forces a faction's goodwill to a specified amount.
This affects their starting goodwill and their current goodwill, forever. They cannot be swayed, no matter what happens.
Ally: 75 or higher Neutral: Between -74 and 74 (inclusive) Hostile: -75 or lower
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.FactionStartingGood:
Start with a specified amount of goodwill with a faction.
This affects their starting goodwill only. Their goodwill will slowly trend back towards their natural goodwill over time.
Ally: 75 or higher Neutral: Between -74 and 74 (inclusive) Hostile: -75 or lower
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.AdditionalUnwaverin: Adds an additional chance for pawns to be unwavering prisoners. When set to 100%, pawns can never be recruited.
+{0} chance for prisoners to be unwaveringly loyal.
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.NoNeutralFactionsEx:
Deselects all neutral factions on the world generation screen, except the selected ones.
This is purely a convenience feature, as you can do the same thing manually on the faction select screen. You can still re-add them manually.
Hidden factions are unaffected.
All neutral factions are deselected by default.
All neutral factions are deselected by default, except for
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.NoHostileFactionsEx:
Deselects all enemy factions on the world generation screen, except the selected ones.
This is purely a convenience feature, as you can do the same thing manually on the faction select screen. You can still re-add them manually.
Hidden factions are unaffected.
All hostile factions are deselected by default.
All hostile factions are deselected by default, except for
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.RemoveFaction.Summa: The following factions are deselected by default
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.RemoveFaction.Help:
Deselects a specific faction on the world generation screen.
This is purely a convenience feature, as you can do the same thing manually on the faction select screen. You can still re-add them manually.
Hidden factions are not selectable.
Prevents you from starting a ship's startup sequence, both for your own starship and the Journey Offer.
The ship can still be researched and built, but it cannot be started, meaning you cannot use it to leave the planet. You may also want to add a Disable Incident part to disable the Journey Offer.
Gravships are unaffected, this is exclusively for ships to the stars (the ones that have the 15-day reactor startup sequence).
Force the selected pawns to have a psylink level.
{0} have a {1} chance to start with a Level {2} Psylink.
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.ForcedImplant.Help: Force the selected pawns to have an implant (such as a bionic limb).
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.ForcedImplant.Summa: {0} have a {1} chance to have a {2} implant.
{0} have a {1} chance to have a {3} {2} implant.
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.ReplaceXenotype.Hel:
If a pawn's xenotype is the chosen "From xenotype" when they are generated, it will be replaced with the chosen "To Xenotype".
Example: replace all Wasters with Dirtmoles.
Does not support custom player-made xenotypes. Instead, use the "Replace xenotype with custom" part, or you can use another mod to export your custom xenotype to a new mod, which will make it selectable here.
You may run into harmless one-time errors when trying to replace a xenotype with a non-violent one (such as Baseliner to Genie), because of various rules like faction leaders needing to be capable of violence. Those pawns may end up still being their original xenotype.
If you stack replacements (Baseliner to Genie and then Genie to Hussar), they will be run top to bottom.
This scenario part may be janky, I recommend something like Xenotype Spawn Control or Worldbuilder instead if you want to get more in-depth.
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.ReplaceXenotype.Sum: {0} have a {1} chance to have {2} xenotype replaced with {3}.
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.ReplaceXenotypeWith:
If a pawn's xenotype is the chosen "From Xenotype" when they are generated, it will be replaced with the chosen "To custom xenotype".
Example: replace all Sanguophages with your own custom version of Sanguophages. (Or something else entirely!)
You may run into harmless one-time errors when trying to replace a xenotype with a non-violent one, because of various rules like faction leaders needing to be capable of violence. Those pawns may end up still being their original xenotype.
{0} have a {1} chance for {2} xenotype to be replaced with custom xenotype {3}.
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.NoColonistRerolls.S: Colonists can't be rerolled during preparation.
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.NoColonistRerolls.H:
Prevents you from rerolling colonists in the preparation screen.
Due to technical limitations, you also cannot change their xenotypes in the preparation screen.
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.WinCondition_Colony: You have successfully established a colony of impressive size!
Having already made it this far, your colony is sure to thrive and survive for many generations. But only time will tell.

Wins the game when you have the specified amount of alive colonists.
Does not count temporary colonists or guests. Does not count unloaded colonists, such as those in cryptosleep or living in outposts from Vanilla Outposts Expanded.
Mind the potential lag when setting this very high. Recommended maximum is 50 on a decent computer. Most vanilla storytellers will stop making it easier to get new colonists when you have around 20 colonists.
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.WinCondition_Conque: Ally with or destroy every other settlement on the planet
Destroy every other settlement on the planet
You have successfully wiped out all your enemies! One by one, your enemies have fallen. You and your allies are now the sole rulers of this planet.
You have successfully wiped out all other settlements on the planet! One by one, your enemies have fallen. You are now the sole ruler of this planet.
No one is left to oppose you.

These soldiers have won the battle, and the war:

{0}

Wins the game when you have wiped out all other settlements except your own.
If "Allow allying" is enabled, you can also ally factions. In this case, you win the game when only your own and allied settlements remain.
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.WinCondition_RoyalT: Have {0} colonists with at least royal title {1} with {2}
You have joined the ranks of nobility and prestige!
Will their legacy last? Only time will tell.

These prestigious individuals will be known for generations to come:

{0}

Wins the game when you have the specified amount of alive colonists with at least the chosen title with the selected faction (usually the Shattered Empire).
Does not count temporary colonists or guests. Does not count unloaded colonists, such as those in cryptosleep or living in outposts from Vanilla Outposts Expanded.
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.WinCondition_Time.W: You have stood the test of time!
Having already made it this far, your colony is sure to thrive and survive for many generations. But only time will tell.
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.WinCondition_Time.H:
Wins the game when time passes the specified amount of days. Default is 600 days, meaning you win the game when you reach Year 10.
You must have at least 1 alive colonist by that point for it to count. Does not count temporary colonists or guests. Does not count unloaded colonists, such as those in cryptosleep or living in outposts from Vanilla Outposts Expanded.
LanguageData.Playwright.ScenParts.WinCondition_SellIt: Perhaps you will stay on this planet, and continue making it big. Or maybe you will venture for greener pastures, ivory towers, or something else entirely. Only time will tell.

Wins the game when you have sold the specified amount of the specified item to traders across your run. This can be any trader.
Example: Sell 5000x Gold over your lifetime.
